decisions.decision: Repo stays private on GitHub by default — flip to public only with a specific reason (Phase 3 distribution being the obvious one, after Phase 2.7 SECURITY.md substantiates the privacy claims)
decisions.rationale: Privacy-first positioning + premature public exposure undermines the brand. User-stated 2026-04-29.

decisions.decision: Phase 2.7 (Security Posture & Government Readiness) added to roadmap, inserted between 2.5 and 3.5
decisions.rationale: User direction 2026-04-29 — broaden audience to include government / defence / institutional buyers; produce auditable SECURITY.md as substantiation for institutional claims

decisions.decision: Audience broadened from 'privacy-conscious individuals' to 6 segments (individuals + government/defence + healthcare/legal + finance/compliance + journalists + air-gapped operators)
decisions.rationale: User direction 2026-04-29; load-bearing differentiator vs Koe / Wispr Flow / cloud dictation broadly

next_action: Run `/gsd:discuss-phase 2.7` to gather context for the Security Posture phase. The phase produces SECURITY.md (threat model + zero-egress verification + SBOM + gap analysis vs NIST SP 800-53 / FIPS 140-3 / FedRAMP / Common Criteria) plus implementation tasks for verification mechanisms (Little Snitch egress proof, SBOM tooling, code signing). Research + verification depth, NOT formal certifications.
context_notes: Phase 2.5 closed cleanly. Working tree empty. Models migrated on host (~466 MB voice-cc → purplevoice). 7/7 tests green. Repo backed up to private GitHub. The .planning/STATE.md follow-ups are all logged (Phase 2 vocab A/B deferred, test_tcc_grep replacement, manual walkthroughs run, Phase 3 STACK.md update, Phase 4 fn-key hotkey schemes). None gate Phase 2.7. Resume cost is low — fresh context window recommended.
